name: Roast Turkey with Grapes and Prunes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly coat the inside of a large, unprinted paper grocery bag with safflower oil.
2. Wash and pat the turkey dry.
3. In a large bowl, mix cornbread crumbs with thyme, sage, and parsley. Set aside.
4. In a saucepan over medium heat, simmer port, prunes, grapes, and apples for 15 minutes. In a skillet over medium-high heat, sauté onion in oil until soft but not browned (8 to 10 minutes). Add the fruit mixture and sautéed onion to the bread crumbs and mix well.
5. Stuff the cavity of the turkey with the bread stuffing. Tie the opening shut with kitchen twine. Dot the exterior of the turkey with small pieces of butter. Place the turkey in the prepared bag, roll up the opening of the bag to seal tightly, and place it in a large roasting pan. Place in the oven and roast for 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
6. During the last 30 minutes, tear away the top of the bag to let the exterior brown. To test for doneness, pierce a leg with the tip of a sharp knife. The juice should spurt out clear yellow; if it is pink, roast the bird for 10 to 15 minutes longer.
Note: If you are concerned about chemicals in the paper of the bag, you may want to line the bottom of the bag with aluminum foil or a rack.
